title: Mixture-of-experts ensemble with fold-safe beta calibration
summary: Public F1 papers emphasize strong regime differences under dry vs wet conditions and severe imbalance; calibration literature shows tree scores are often miscalibrated, and beta calibration can outperform plain Platt scaling on skewed score distributions.
rationale: Public F1 papers emphasize strong regime differences under dry vs wet conditions and severe imbalance; calibration literature shows tree scores are often miscalibrated, and beta calibration can outperform plain Platt scaling on skewed score distributions. A single global model may blur dry, wet/intermediate, and late-race pit logic.
implementation_hint: Train specialized experts such as dry compounds, wet/intermediate, and perhaps early/mid/late-race or first-stint vs later-stint models. Produce OOF probabilities, calibrate each expert with beta or isotonic-on-OOF, then blend with a simple meta-model or weighted average. Keep the final objective AUC-focused, but inspect calibration to stabilize ensemble behavior.
expected_effect: Usually modest standalone gains, but often strong as the final increment once base models are close; especially useful if different regimes have different score shapes.
risk: Extra moving parts can overfit if regime splits are too fine or calibration is fit on in-fold predictions.
